items.title: VI Live: Inter op rand van afgrond, Club Brugge ruikt bloed tegen Atlético
items.summary: Internazionale hoopt dinsdagavond de 3-1 uitnederlaag tegen FK Bodø/Glimt weg te poetsen. Verder heeft Club Brugge op bezoek bij Atlético Madrid kans om de achtste finale te halen. Volg de duels in de tussenronde van de Champions League in deze VI Live.

items.title: Nieuwe spelregels op komst om spelhervattingen sneller te laten verlopen
items.summary: De spelregelcommissie International Football Association Board (IFAB) gaat zaterdag tijdens een vergadering in Wales stemmen over nieuwe spelregels rondom doeltrappen, ingooien en wisselmomenten.

items.title: Bodø/Glimt tart alle logica: van 0,3 procent kans naar Europese sensatie
items.summary: Als de ' hel ' opnieuw bevriest, heet die tegenwoordig Aspmyra. FK Bodø/Glimt blijft de Champions League op zijn kop zetten. Na de 3-1 zege op Internazionale in de heenwedstrijd van de play-offs dromen ze in Noorwegen hardop van meer. En dat terwijl de statistieken hen maanden geleden al hadden afgeschreven.

items.title: Wat zijn de FIFA Series, wanneer zijn ze en waar worden ze gehouden?
items.summary: Op woensdag 25 maart gaat de tweede editie van de FIFA Series van start. Dat is een soort vriendschappelijk oefentoernooi voor landenteams. In dit artikel lees je alles wat je moet weten over de FIFA Series 2026.

items.title: Spurs plan to rip up wage structure and invest in squad if club avoid relegation
items.summary: Tottenham have lowest wage bill among ‘big six’ sides Major overhaul of squad planned for summer by owners Tottenham’s owners are planning to rip up the club’s rigid wage structure before a major overhaul of the squad this summer if the team avoid relegation. There is an acknowledgment at Spurs that the club have underinvested in player salaries for years, which along with an injury crisis has led to their being 16th in the Premier League, four points off the bottom three. Continue reading...

items.title: Transfers Feyenoord: dossiers Read en Hadj Moussa wachten op Van Wonderen
items.summary: VI volgt het transfernieuws bij Feyenoord op de voet. De doelwitten van de Rotterdammers, duiding van de clubwatcher, of aandacht in buitenlandse media. We verzamelen alles hier.

items.title: Mace: Why Everton is the perfect place for me after Man City struggles
items.summary: "When I came to Everton, I felt so welcomed, so valued and to this day, it's definitely the best decision I ever made in my career."

items.title: Sources: Mexico game still on amid cartel violence
items.summary: Mexico plans to proceed with its friendly match scheduled against Iceland on Wednesday at the Corregidora Stadium in Queretaro amid unrest and violence in the country, sources told ESPN.

items.title: Source: Orlando in talks with Atleti's Griezmann
items.summary: Orlando City SC is in talks to sign Atlético Madrid attacker Antoine Griezmann, a source told ESPN.

items.title: Lammens mitigates 'chaos' as United reach top 4
items.summary: Manchester United head coach Michael Carrick had high praise for goalkeeper Senne Lammens after a 1-0 win against Everton in the Premier League on Monday.

items.title: Benfica's Prestianni suspended for Madrid tie
items.summary: UEFA has provisionally suspended Benfica's Gianluca Prestianni for Wednesday's Champions League match against Real Madrid following allegations that he used racist language directed at Vinícius Junior in last week's first leg.

items.title: Howe’s Newcastle have shown European swagger but may need stylistic switch
items.summary: Manager has already undergone a tactical evolution but needs further progress if his side seek highest honours At first glance, Anthony Gordon appears to have little in common with Sir Keir Starmer but, like the prime minister, the Newcastle forward looks infinitely more surefooted on foreign soil than domestic battlegrounds. In the Champions League, Gordon has scored 10 goals in nine games. In the Premier League, meanwhile, he has managed a modest three in 21 appearances, two of which were penalties. Whether deployed wide on the left or, following a recent positional shift, at centre-forward, Gordon seems emblematic of a wider Newcastle paradox. Just like Eddie Howe’s team, he is irrepressible one match and ineffective the next. Continue reading...

items.title: Mr Nugget bids to regain winning thread at Southwell
items.summary: We have a busy day’s racing on Tuesday with All-Weather action from Newcastle and Southwell, live on Sky Sports Racing.

items.title: Can Aston Martin recover from nightmare F1 pre-season?
items.summary: Having approached Formula 1's major 2026 regulation change as the team that was most confident of breaking into the sport's established elite, Aston Martin have endured a disastrous pre-season that has left their short to mid-term goals in apparent tatters.

items.title: Quiz - name every Englishman to score a Champions League hat-trick
items.summary: In Newcastle's match with Qarabag, a certain winger became the 11th Englishmen to score a Champions League treble.

items.title: Storylines to follow as Super League hits Las Vegas 2026
items.summary: Ahead of rugby league's trip to Las Vegas for 2026, Sky Sports' Megan Wellens takes you through some of the key storylines to follow from Hull KR and Leeds Rhinos...

items.title: Man United's successful summer signings provide hope for the future
items.summary: Manchester United are still looking to qualify for the Champions League next season, and the success of their summer signings might help them get there.

items.title: Bradley hits record-breaking 384-yard drive at TGL
items.summary: Keegan Bradley set the record for the longest drive recorded at TGL with a huge 384-yards.

items.title: Sesko and Lammens allow Man Utd to dream of Champions League
items.summary: Striker Benjamin Sesko and goalkeeper Senne Lammens are the key figures as Manchester United gain a hugely valuable Premier League win at Everton.

items.title: Who has made Troy's Premier League team of the week?
items.summary: After every round of Premier League matches this season, Troy Deeney gives us his team of the week. Do you agree with his choices?
